candidates.name: OpenClaw
why_candidate: OpenClaw exposes a broad autonomous-agent surface with Skills, MCP integrations and remote channels.
An OpenClaw-like profile and validation fixtures are available.

data_needs.description: Collect repository, registry and public community activity signals.
Collect repository URL, stars, forks, release history, issue activity and maintainer signals.
Inventory common OpenClaw skills, MCP integrations and install paths.

profile_impacts: Add common OpenClaw skill and MCP replacement recommendations.
notes: Popularity and safety conclusions require reproducible evidence.

candidates.name: Hermes Agent
why_candidate: Hermes Agent includes optional MCPs, Skills, gateways, scheduled tasks and memory surfaces.
A Hermes-like profile and validation fixtures are available.

data_needs.description: Collect repository, registry and public community activity signals.
Collect repository URL, stars, forks, release history, issue activity and maintainer signals.
Read public Hermes Agent architecture and identify product-specific config paths and risky behaviors.

profile_impacts: Add migration-specific rules for imported skills and memories.
Add gateway and scheduled task recommendations.
notes: The current Hermes-like profile is intentionally conservative and marked draft.

candidates.name: OpenManus
why_candidate: OpenManus is part of the broader open autonomous-agent landscape.
May represent a different architecture from OpenClaw-like and Hermes-like environments.

data_needs.description: Identify canonical repository, docs and install methods.
Collect GitHub, search and community activity signals.
Map config paths, skill paths, tool registry paths and execution behavior.

profile_impacts: Decide whether OpenManus needs a dedicated profile or can use generic-agent plus MCP and skill profiles.
notes: No popularity or safety conclusion has been recorded yet.

candidates.name: Lobster-like Agent Ecosystem
why_candidate: This ecosystem represents autonomous agents with Skills and MCP-style integrations.
Useful for checking whether the architecture generalizes beyond OpenClaw and Hermes.

data_needs.description: Identify canonical repositories, docs and common install paths.
Inventory common skills, browser tools and MCP resources.
Check whether existing security or guard tools already target this ecosystem.

profile_impacts: Use research findings to decide whether a lobster-like profile is warranted for Phase 1.
notes: This candidate should not be prioritized only because it is structurally convenient.

candidates.name: MCP Registry Ecosystem
why_candidate: MCP integrations are a cross-agent risk and recommendation surface.
A reusable MCP reputation layer can benefit multiple agent profiles.

data_needs.description: Identify public MCP registries, common MCP server lists and high-usage MCP repositories.
Classify MCP tools into filesystem, shell, browser, network, credential and database access.
Map risky MCP patterns to safer local, pinned or read-only alternatives.

profile_impacts: Feed all agent profiles with shared MCP rules and recommendations.
Create MCP-specific reputation and replacement data.
notes: This is likely a shared layer rather than a single-agent profile.
